meta.title: Old Exam Examples
meta.description: Mixed questions from past DWP exams including true/false, Express, Fetch API, and more.

questions.questionMd: The order in which callbacks are added to the event queue determines the order in which they are executed.
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: True

questions.questionMd: Express.js is a server-side JavaScript framework.
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: True

questions.questionMd: What is a server-side programming language?

options.label: A
options.text: A programming language that can run on both the client-side and server-side

options.label: B
options.text: A programming language that runs on the server-side

options.label: C
options.text: A programming language that runs on the client-side

questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: A programming language that runs on the server-side

questions.questionMd: In Node.js, the `require` function is used to import external modules and libraries.
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: True

questions.questionMd: What does the HTTP method PUT do in RESTful API?
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: Update an existing resource

questions.questionMd: When an asynchronous function is called, where does it get executed?
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: The callback queue

questions.questionMd: Which of the following correctly completes the code to create an Express app?
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: express()

questions.questionMd: Which of the following methods is used to specify the URL path and HTTP method for a route in Express.js?
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: app.get()

questions.questionMd: Which method is used to parse the response body as JSON?
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: json()

questions.questionMd: What is the purpose of the `headers` property in a Fetch API request?

options.label: A
options.text: To specify the URL of the server to send the request to

options.label: B
options.text: To specify the content type of the request body

options.label: C
options.text: To specify the type of HTTP method to use

questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: To specify the content type of the request body. This source answer is preserved as shown in the PDF.

questions.questionMd: The event loop is a feature of the V8 engine used by Google Chrome and Node.js.
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: True

questions.questionMd: What is a server?

options.label: A
options.text: A computer program that processes requests and delivers data over the internet

options.label: B
options.text: A programming language used for creating web applications

options.label: C
options.text: A type of database used for storing user information

questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: A computer program that processes requests and delivers data over the internet

questions.questionMd: What is the syntax for making a GET request with the Fetch API?
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: fetch(url, {method: 'GET'})

questions.questionMd: In Express.js, the `res.sendFile()` method is used to send a file as a response.
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: True

questions.questionMd: What is the difference between `fetch` and `fetch with async/await`?

options.label: A
options.text: `fetch` with async/await is a deprecated method of handling responses

options.label: B
options.text: `fetch` with async/await is a more concise and readable way to handle responses

options.label: C
options.text: There is no difference, they are the same thing

questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: `fetch` with async/await is a more concise and readable way to handle responses

questions.questionMd: Express is a Node.js web framework for building web applications and APIs.
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: True

questions.questionMd: The event loop can block the execution of synchronous code in JavaScript.
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: False

questions.questionMd: Which of the following statements is true about Express.js?

options.label: A
options.text: It is a programming language for Node.js

options.label: B
options.text: It is a web application framework for Node.js

options.label: C
options.text: It is a package manager for Node.js

options.label: D
options.text: It is a database management system for Node.js

questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: It is a web application framework for Node.js

questions.questionMd: The `app.listen` method is used to start a Node.js server with Express.
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: True

questions.questionMd: What is the event loop in JavaScript?

options.label: A
options.text: A system that handles asynchronous operations in JavaScript

options.label: B
options.text: A function that synchronously executes JavaScript code

options.label: C
options.text: A loop that executes JavaScript code repeatedly

questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: A system that handles asynchronous operations in JavaScript

questions.questionMd: What is the purpose of the `req` object in Express.js?

options.label: A
options.text: To receive data from the client

options.label: B
options.text: To store data in the client's browser

options.label: D
options.text: To send a response to the client

questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: To receive data from the client

questions.questionMd: Express.js can be used to create both RESTful and non-RESTful APIs.
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: True

questions.questionMd: The call stack is a first-in, last-out data structure that stores function calls.
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: True

questions.questionMd: Which status code indicates a successful response in RESTful API?
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: 200 OK

questions.questionMd: What is the back-end of a web application?

options.label: A
options.text: The part of the application the user interacts with

options.label: B
options.text: The part of the application responsible for processing and storing data

options.label: C
options.text: The part of the application responsible for designing the user interface

questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: The part of the application responsible for processing and storing data

questions.questionMd: Which of the following is a difference between Node.js and Express.js?

options.label: A
options.text: Node.js is a package manager, while Express.js is a web application framework

options.label: B
options.text: Node.js is a runtime environment for executing JavaScript, while Express.js is a web application framework built on top of Node.js

options.label: C
options.text: Node.js is used for server-side scripting, while Express.js is used for client-side scripting

options.label: D
options.text: Node.js is a programming language, while Express.js is a database management system

questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: Node.js is a runtime environment for executing JavaScript, while Express.js is a web application framework built on top of Node.js

questions.questionMd: What is the purpose of the `res.send()` method in an Express.js application?

options.label: A
options.text: To send a response back to the client

options.label: B
options.text: To define the routes for the application

options.label: C
options.text: To handle errors in the application

questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: To send a response back to the client

questions.questionMd: What is the purpose of the event loop in Node.js?
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: To handle asynchronous operations

questions.questionMd: What is the purpose of the `app.use()` middleware function in Express.js?
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: To execute a middleware function. Some other source screenshots in this PDF specifically mention static-file serving via express.static(), but this question's displayed source answer is preserved.

questions.questionMd: What is the purpose of the `npm` command in Node.js?

options.label: B
options.text: To install and manage packages and dependencies

options.label: C
options.text: To create a new Node.js application

questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: To install and manage packages and dependencies

questions.questionMd: The event loop is only used in web browsers and is not present in server-side JavaScript environments like Node.js.
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: False

questions.questionMd: Node.js is a server-side runtime environment for executing JavaScript code.
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: True

questions.questionMd: What is the purpose of the `express.static()` middleware in Express.js?
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: To serve static files

questions.questionMd: In Node.js, asynchronous operations can be handled using callbacks, promises, or async/await.
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: True

questions.questionMd: Which of the following is used on the client-side to make HTTP requests?
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: Fetch API

questions.questionMd: What is the Fetch API used for?

options.label: A
options.text: To create and manipulate HTML elements

options.label: B
options.text: To make HTTP requests and receive responses from servers

questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: To make HTTP requests and receive responses from servers

questions.questionMd: Express.js can only handle HTTP requests and responses.
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: True. This source answer is preserved from the PDF.

questions.questionMd: The event loop in JavaScript is a single-threaded system.
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: True

questions.questionMd: The event loop is responsible for handling both synchronous and asynchronous operations in JavaScript.
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: True

questions.questionMd: Express.js allows you to serve static files, such as HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, using the `express.static()` middleware.
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: True

questions.questionMd: What is a Promise in the context of the Fetch API?

options.label: A
options.text: A way to handle errors that occur during a Fetch request

options.label: B
options.text: A way to handle synchronous operations in JavaScript

options.label: C
options.text: A way to handle asynchronous operations in JavaScript

questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: A way to handle asynchronous operations in JavaScript

questions.questionMd: What is the purpose of the `express()` function in Express.js?

options.label: A
options.text: It sends a response to the client

options.label: B
options.text: It creates a new Express application

questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: It creates a new Express application

questions.questionMd: What is a RESTful API?

options.label: A
options.text: An API that uses the REST architectural style for building web services

options.label: B
options.text: An API that is easy to use and understand

options.label: C
options.text: An API that is designed specifically for mobile applications

questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: An API that uses the REST architectural style for building web services

questions.questionMd: Express.js can be used as a substitute for Node.js.
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: False

questions.questionMd: What is the purpose of the `app.listen()` method in an Express.js application?

options.label: A
options.text: To start the server and listen for incoming requests

options.label: B
options.text: To define the routes for the application

options.label: C
options.text: To handle errors in the application

questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: To start the server and listen for incoming requests

questions.questionMd: What is the purpose of the `response` object in a Fetch API request?

options.label: A
options.text: To represent the request to the server

options.label: B
options.text: To represent the response from the server

options.label: C
options.text: To represent the data sent to the server

questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: To represent the response from the server

questions.questionMd: What is npm?

options.label: C
options.text: A framework for building web applications

questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: A package manager for Node.js

questions.questionMd: What is a web server?

options.label: A
options.text: A computer program that delivers web pages and other resources to clients

options.label: B
options.text: A type of database used for storing user information

options.label: C
options.text: A programming language used for creating web applications

questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: A computer program that delivers web pages and other resources to clients

questions.questionMd: What is Node.js?

options.label: A
options.text: A server-side runtime environment for executing JavaScript code

options.label: B
options.text: A client-side runtime environment for executing JavaScript code

questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: A server-side runtime environment for executing JavaScript code

questions.questionMd: What is an API?

options.label: A
options.text: A type of database used for storing user information

options.label: B
options.text: A programming language used for creating web applications

options.label: C
options.text: A set of protocols and tools used for building software applications

questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: A set of protocols and tools used for building software applications

questions.questionMd: Which of the following correctly completes the code to send a response with JSON data in Express?
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: json

questions.questionMd: What does the HTTP method GET do in RESTful API?
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: Retrieve a resource

questions.questionMd: Which of the following is used on the server-side to handle HTTP requests?
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: Express.js

questions.questionMd: Which of the following is a way to access request parameters in Express.js?
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: req.params

questions.questionMd: In Express.js, the `res.json()` method is used to send a JSON response to the client.
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: True

questions.questionMd: Which of the following correctly completes the missing code in this Node.js example that creates an HTTP server?
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: res.end('Hello World!');

questions.questionMd: What is the default value for the `mode` property in a Fetch API request?
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: "no-cors". This answer is preserved exactly as shown in the PDF source.

questions.questionMd: Express.js routes can only handle GET requests.
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: False

questions.questionMd: What is the purpose of the `http` module in Node.js?
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: To perform network operations

questions.questionMd: Which of the following correctly completes the code to define a route parameter in Express?
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: id

questions.questionMd: Which of the following is NOT a key concept in understanding the event loop?
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: V8 JavaScript Engine

questions.questionMd: What is a framework?

options.label: A
options.text: A collection of pre-written code and tools used for building applications

options.label: B
options.text: A type of database used for storing user information

options.label: C
options.text: A programming language used for creating web applications

questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: A collection of pre-written code and tools used for building applications

questions.questionMd: What is HTTP?

options.label: A
options.text: A protocol used for transmitting data over the internet

options.label: B
options.text: A type of database used for storing user information

options.label: C
options.text: A programming language used for creating web applications

questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: A protocol used for transmitting data over the internet

questions.questionMd: What does the HTTP method POST do in RESTful API?
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: Create a new resource

questions.questionMd: What does the HTTP method DELETE do in RESTful API?
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: Delete a resource

questions.questionMd: What does a RESTful API return by default?
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: JSON data

questions.questionMd: Which HTTP methods are commonly used in RESTful API?
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: GET, POST, DELETE, PUT

questions.questionMd: Which of the following is an example of an event in JavaScript?
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: A mouse click

questions.questionMd: What does the Fetch API return?

options.label: A
options.text: A string representing the response body

options.label: C
options.text: A Promise that resolves to a Response object

questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: A Promise that resolves to a Response object

questions.questionMd: What is the purpose of the `Headers` object in a Fetch API request?

options.label: A
options.text: To represent the headers of the response

options.label: B
options.text: To represent the headers of the request

options.label: C
options.text: To represent the data sent to the server

questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: To represent the headers of the request

questions.questionMd: What is the purpose of the `then` method in a Fetch API request?

options.label: A
options.text: To handle the response data asynchronously

options.label: B
options.text: To handle errors that occur during the request

options.label: C
options.text: To convert the response to JSON format

questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: To handle the response data asynchronously

questions.questionMd: What is a Promise object?

options.label: A
options.text: A way to handle errors that occur during a Fetch request

options.label: B
options.text: A way to handle synchronous operations in JavaScript

options.label: C
options.text: A way to handle asynchronous operations in JavaScript

questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: A way to handle asynchronous operations in JavaScript

questions.questionMd: What is the purpose of the `body` property in a Fetch API request?

options.label: A
options.text: To specify the type of HTTP method to use

options.label: B
options.text: To specify the content of the request

options.label: C
options.text: To specify the URL of the server to send the request to

questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: To specify the content of the request

questions.questionMd: The event loop is responsible for managing the order in which code is executed in JavaScript.
questions.explanationMd: Correct answer according to the source material: True
